campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary local rule and moving forward with plans to build a bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including applying for grant funding to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth recreation scholarships from Water Tower Kitchen
Extension of temporary ordinance number 2326
Grant application for Hamilton Avenue bicycle bridge over Highway 17
Final feasibility study approved for Highway 17 bicycle overcrossing

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on a new rule or law (ordinance) that they're planning to adopt. After hearing from the public, they moved forward with introducing the ordinance and plan to formally vote on it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community input on a new ordinance
First reading of ordinance waived to speed up the process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at next council meeting
Details of the specific ordinance topic not specified in agenda

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from several March sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was primarily focused on administrative housekeeping and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six March meetings (regular, special, and closed sessions)
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize their upcoming agendas for late April. They reviewed which new items to add to the April 21st and April 28th meetings, decided if any agenda items needed specific time slots, and checked whether any documents needed special approval notices.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 21, 2026 City Council agenda
Planned the April 28, 2026 draft agenda
Reviewed public records from April 2-9, 2026
VEBA Advisory Committee matters
Scheduled specific time slots for important agenda items

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held two meetings on April 14th. First, they studied the city's building and infrastructure plans for 2026-27 to decide where to spend money on improvements. Then they officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in Mountain View.
keyTopics: 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program budget and project planning
Teen Month proclamation
National Volunteer Week recognition

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review their previous meeting notes from March and introduced a new rule about tenant protections in Sunnyvale. The meeting focused on housing issues and how the city can better support residents.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25 meeting minutes
New tenant protection rules being proposed for renters
Updates to Chapter 19.71 of city housing codes
Housing and human services policy review

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 16, 2026 where residents were invited to speak about any city matters for up to 3 minutes each. The council also held elections for Chair and Vice Chair positions and discussed the upcoming fiscal year budget.
keyTopics: Public comment period - residents can speak on any city issue (3 minutes max)
Chair and Vice Chair elections for the council
Fiscal Year budget development and presentation
Note: Council cannot make decisions on topics not officially on the agenda

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal information for residents: if you disagree with a city decision and want to sue or challenge it in court, you have 90 days from when the decision becomes final to file your lawsuit. This is a standard legal notice to make sure residents know their rights and deadlines.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to file lawsuits against city decisions
Legal challenges to quasi-adjudicative (decision-making) actions
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 requirements
How residents can appeal city council determinations

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle routine business including reviewing how federal housing and community development money will be spent over the next few years, looking at new project ideas from individual council members, and considering changes to the process for filling council vacancies. The council also approved meeting minutes and previewed upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Federal funding plan updates for housing and community development (2025-2030)
New project requests from individual council members
Changes to how council vacancies get filled
Upcoming meeting schedule and agenda items

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The owner wants to officially designate it as a historic building, which would allow them to add 5,000 extra square feet of floor space as a reward for preserving and upgrading the structure to meet earthquake safety standards.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade (Category 4 to Category 2)
5,000 square feet bonus floor area for historic and seismic improvements
Downtown Commercial zone development
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule updates
March 12, 2026 meeting minutes approval
